paper_title: Branch, or Layer? Zeroth-Order Optimization for Continual Learning of Vision-Language Models

architectures.description: Introduces unlabeled reference dataset for knowledge distillation
Maintains consistency in multimodal representation spaces through loss functions

optimization_strategies.description: Compares FO optimization strategies and finds ZO advantages in alleviating catastrophic forgetting

approaches.name: ZSCL
approaches.description: Introduces unlabeled reference dataset for knowledge distillation to guide parameter space optimization

approaches.name: Mod-X
approaches.description: Maintains consistency in multimodal representation spaces through specially designed loss functions

approaches.name: MoE4Adapter
approaches.description: Adopts PEFT strategies to reduce computational overhead while maintaining competitive performance

approaches.name: MeZO and MaZO
approaches.description: Leverage ZO optimization to address memory challenges in fine-tuning large language models

approaches.name: ZeroFlow
approaches.description: Systematically compares FO optimization strategies and discovers ZO advantages in alleviating catastrophic forgetting

investigations.name: Investigation 1
investigations.question: Can ZO and FO collaborate for global model optimization across branches?
investigations.method: Implement ZO in either the visual or linguistic modality branch while retaining FO in the other
investigations.result: Significantly outperforms the naive full-ZO approach

investigations.name: Investigation 2
investigations.question: Can ZO and FO collaborate for hierarchical optimization within branch layers?
investigations.method: Deploy ZO only in specific layers (e.g., odd or consecutive layers) within a single branch while keeping FO in the remaining layers
investigations.result: Yields better results, with certain configurations surpassing the full-FO baseline

stabilization_mechanism.benefit: Improves optimization convergence and overall continual learning performance

title: Branch, or Layer? Zeroth-Order Optimization for Continual Learning of Vision-Language Models
